claimText: The epigenome consists of chemical compounds that modify or regulate gene expression.
evidenceExcerpt: Epigenome consists of chemical compounds that modify or regulate gene expression.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and function of the epigenome.
distractorHooks: Epigenome alters the underlying DNA sequence
Epigenome consists of structural proteins that protect DNA from mutations

claimText: Epigenome editing controls gene expression without cutting the underlying DNA.
evidenceExcerpt: Epigenome editing allows for precise control over gene expression with reversible changes without cutting DNA
examUse: Comparison question between traditional gene editing (which cuts DNA) and epigenome editing.
distractorHooks: Epigenome editing involves cutting and replacing DNA segments
Epigenome editing permanently deletes target genes

claimText: Changes made to gene expression via epigenome editing are reversible.
evidenceExcerpt: Epigenome editing allows for precise control over gene expression with reversible changes
examUse: Statement-based question on the characteristics and safety profile of epigenome editing.
distractorHooks: Changes made via epigenome editing are permanent and irreversible
Epigenome editing causes irreversible mutations in the genome
